description_long: This is a large, elongated barn structure with a steeply pitched, symmetrical gable roof that extends nearly to the ground, reminiscent of traditional Scandinavian or Viking longhouses. The roof is covered with overlapping shingles, and the eaves project outward, providing a sheltered walkway supported by a series of evenly spaced vertical posts. The front and rear gables are clad in vertical wooden planks, with a prominent triangular peak and a small decorative ridge ornament at the apex. The barn sits on a rectangular base that appears to be stone or packed earth, and the open lower section suggests a covered porch or storage area. There are no visible windows, and the main entrance is a wide, open area beneath the front gable, suitable for large equipment or livestock.
description: A long, steeply gabled barn with shingled roof, vertical wood siding, and a covered porch supported by posts. The structure is reminiscent of a traditional Scandinavian longhouse.
description_view_0: Front view showing the triangular gable, open entrance, and covered porch supported by posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, shingled roof and the row of supporting posts along the length of the barn.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a similar gable and open lower section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the extended roof and supporting posts.
attribute_door_style: single wide open entrance under the front gable, no visible doors
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
